messages.subject: [R-sig-Epi] Registration and abstract submission is now OPEN for Statistical Analysis of Multi-outcome Data Conference (SAM 2019) in Manchester
messages.subject_clean: Registration and abstract submission is now OPEN for Statistical Analysis of Multi-outcome Data Conference (SAM 2019) in Manchester
messages.body_plain:
***** With apologies for cross posting *****



Registration and abstract submission is now OPEN for the Statistical Analysis of Multi-Outcome Data Conference 2019 (SAM 2019).



The conference will take place at the University of Manchester on the 6thand 7thJune 2019, with a workshop on Mendelian Randomisation preceding the meeting on the afternoon of the 5th. Please note: places on the workshop are limited.



The meeting will cover a wide range of topics, with areas of interest including:



?       Joint Modelling of Longitudinal and Time-to-Event Data



?       Multivariate Longitudinal Data and Survival



?       Multistate Models and Competing Risks



?       Risk Prediction



?       Causal Analysis



?       Novel Approaches to Modelling Longitudinal / Survival Data



?       Recurrent Events



?      Multivariate Survival and Prediction.



Confirmed speakers: Laura Bonnett (University of Liverpool), Philip Dawid (University of Cambridge), Harvey Goldstein (University of Bristol) and Fiona Steele (London School of Economics).



Abstracts should be submitted to sam2019 at manchester.ac.uk<mailto:sam2019 at manchester.ac.uk<mailto:sam2019 at manchester.ac.uk%3cmailto:sam2019 at manchester.ac.uk>> by 21st March.



Early bird rates of ?230 (student) and ?250 (non-student) are available until this date.



Website: https://sites.google.com/view/sam2019/home



Registration link: https://estore.manchester.ac.uk/conferences-and-events/school-of-mathematics/centre-for-biostatistics/statistical-analysis-of-multioutcome-data-sam-2019



Please direct any queries to SAM2019 at manchester.ac.uk<mailto:SAM2019 at manchester.ac.uk<mailto:SAM2019 at manchester.ac.uk%3cmailto:SAM2019 at manchester.ac.uk>>



Thanks


Katie Stocking
Medical Statistician
Centre for Biostatistics
Faculty of Biology, Medicine and Health
The University of Manchester




	[[alternative HTML version deleted]]
